title: Admin Console — Command Parsing
description: Create an AdminConsole class that provides an interactive admin interface for the server. Parse commands using regex: 'status' (show server status), 'set key value' (store a value), 'get key' (retrieve a value), 'list' (list all keys), 'shutdown' (stop the server), and 'help' (show available commands). Include a command history (list of past commands) and tab-completion support (return matching commands from a known list given a prefix). Methods: parse_command(text) returns a dict with 'command' and 'args', execute(text) parses and returns a result string, get_history() returns the command history list, and complete(prefix) returns matching command names.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 10: AdminConsole
import os
import tempfile

assert 'AdminConsole' in dir(), 'AdminConsole class must be defined'

# Create a DataStore for testing
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
    db_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.db')
    store = DataStore(db_path)
    monitor = HealthMonitor()
    console = AdminConsole(data_store=store, health_monitor=monitor)

    # Test required methods
    assert callable(getattr(console, 'parse_command', None)), 'Must have parse_command'
    assert callable(getattr(console, 'execute', None)), 'Must have execute'
    assert callable(getattr(console, 'get_history', None)), 'Must have get_history'
    assert callable(getattr(console, 'complete', None)), 'Must have complete'

    # Test parse_command
    parsed = console.parse_command('set name Alice')
    assert isinstance(parsed, dict), 'parse_command should return a dict'
    assert parsed['command'] == 'set', f"Command should be 'set', got {parsed['command']}"
    assert parsed['args'] == ['name', 'Alice'], f"Args should be ['name', 'Alice'], got {parsed['args']}"

    parsed = console.parse_command('status')
    assert parsed['command'] == 'status', 'Command should be status'
    assert parsed['args'] == [], 'status should have no args'

    parsed = console.parse_command('')
    assert parsed['command'] is None, 'Empty input should have command=None'

    # Test execute - set and get
    result = console.execute('set color blue')
    assert isinstance(result, str), 'execute should return a string'

    result = console.execute('get color')
    assert 'blue' in result, f'get color should return blue, got {result}'

    # Test execute - list
    result = console.execute('list')
    assert 'color' in result, f'list should include color, got {result}'

    # Test execute - status
    result = console.execute('status')
    assert isinstance(result, str), 'status should return a string'

    # Test execute - help
    result = console.execute('help')
    assert 'status' in result, 'help should mention status command'
    assert 'set' in result, 'help should mention set command'

    # Test execute - shutdown
    result = console.execute('shutdown')
    assert 'shutdown' in result.lower() or 'shutting' in result.lower(), 'shutdown should confirm'

    # Test execute - unknown command
    result = console.execute('foobar')
    assert 'unknown' in result.lower() or 'Unknown' in result, 'Should report unknown command'

    # Test command history
    history = console.get_history()
    assert isinstance(history, list), 'get_history should return a list'
    assert len(history) >= 5, f'History should have at least 5 entries, got {len(history)}'
    assert 'set color blue' in history, 'History should include executed commands'

    # Test tab completion
    matches = console.complete('s')
    assert isinstance(matches, list), 'complete should return a list'
    assert 'set' in matches, 'Completing "s" should include set'
    assert 'status' in matches, 'Completing "s" should include status'
    assert 'shutdown' in matches, 'Completing "s" should include shutdown'

    matches = console.complete('se')
    assert matches == ['set'], f'Completing "se" should return ["set"], got {matches}'

    matches = console.complete('xyz')
    assert matches == [], 'Completing "xyz" should return empty list'
acceptance_criteria: AdminConsole accepts optional data_store and health_monitor in constructor
parse_command(text) returns a dict with 'command' and 'args' keys
execute(text) handles all 6 commands: status, set, get, list, shutdown, help
execute returns appropriate string results for each command
Unknown commands return an error message
get_history() returns a list of all executed command strings
complete(prefix) returns matching command names from the COMMANDS list
hints: Use text.strip().split() to break the command into parts. parts[0] is the command, parts[1:] are args.
For parse_command, handle empty/whitespace input by returning {'command': None, 'args': []}.
In execute(), use if/elif to dispatch based on parsed['command'].
For complete(), use a list comprehension: [cmd for cmd in self.COMMANDS if cmd.startswith(prefix)]
